name: E2M1 (MXFP4)
oneliner: 4-bit micro-float: only 15 distinct values, made viable by sharing an 8-bit block scale across 32 elements. Next-gen format for Blackwell GPUs.
explanation: Part of the OCP Microscaling (MX) spec (AMD, ARM, Intel, Meta, Microsoft, NVIDIA, Qualcomm, 2023). Standalone E2M1 is nearly useless — 15 values. The MX trick: 32 E2M1 elements share one E8M0 scale (8-bit exponent, no mantissa). Scale handles magnitude, E2M1 handles relative differences within the block. 2$\times$ compute density vs fp8. NVIDIA Blackwell has native MXFP4 Tensor Cores. Also defines MXFP6 (E3M2/E2M3) and MXFP8.
fundamentals: E2M1: 1 sign | 2 exp (bias 1) | 1 mantissa. No inf/NaN. Positive values: 0, 0.5, 1.0, 1.5, 2.0, 3.0, 4.0, 6.0. With sign: 15 distinct values. E8M0 block scale: pure power of 2 (2^(exp-127)), range 2^-127 to $2^{127}$. Effective value = E2M1_value $\times$ 2^(shared_exp - 127). Effective bpw: 4 + 8/32 = 4.25 bits.

foundational_papers.title: OCP Microscaling Formats (MX) Specification v1.0
foundational_papers.authors: OCP (AMD, ARM, Intel, Meta, Microsoft, NVIDIA, Qualcomm)

foundational_papers.title: Microscaling Data Formats for Deep Learning
